meta.note: Trusted source list (spec §7). Editable only by Nicole. Per the spec: 'A single use by a trusted source is not sufficient on its own. The two-source rule still applies. The trusted list is a confidence weight, not a bypass.' Sources match by canonical domain — Lexi's source extraction normalizes URLs to domain-only before checking. The list reviews quarterly per spec §7; Lexi proposes additions/removals via Notes for Nicole and Nicole approves.

sources.label: Simon Willison's Weblog
sources.rationale: Independent technical commentator. Citation-heavy, links generously, often the first place a term appears in serious technical use. Already cited in 4 of 7 current must-reads; consistently high signal for 'is this term being used by practitioners.'

sources.label: Normaltech (Kapoor & Narayanan)
sources.rationale: Arvind Narayanan and Sayash Kapoor's primary substack. Academic rigor + willingness to call out hype. Source of capability-reliability gap, open-world evaluations, and agent-reliability framings now in the graph or longlist.

sources.label: AI Snake Oil
sources.rationale: Same author cluster as normaltech.ai (Kapoor & Narayanan) but a more journalistic register. Trusted-listing both is a deliberate choice to weight the skeptical-academic voice — note that the auditor still treats single-author clusters as a source-pattern signal independently.

sources.label: Stratechery (Ben Thompson)
sources.rationale: Independent (subscription-funded — no advertiser pressure), reasoned, willing to dissent from consensus. Strong on the strategy/economics angle of AI. Source of the trainium promotion and several infrastructure-tier framings.

sources.label: Harvard Business Review
sources.rationale: Peer-business writing on AI strategy and management. The venue where business framings of AI tend to get formal treatment. Trusted only when the article is AI-relevant — Lexi's extract prompt rejects HBR's broad non-AI coverage; trust-weight applies on the surviving subset. Fetched via the Feedburner mirror in config.mjs.

sources.label: MIT Sloan Management Review
sources.rationale: Academic-business venue, peer-reviewed-ish. Complements HBR with more research-grounded angle. Added to config.mjs sources alongside this trusted-listing so the weight has somewhere to land.

sources.label: Import AI (Jack Clark)
sources.rationale: Jack Clark's weekly newsletter (former OpenAI policy lead, Anthropic co-founder). Synthesizes ~30 papers/week with editorial framing. Now-independent voice (not Anthropic content despite the founder connection). Added to config.mjs sources alongside this trusted-listing.
